**Status: Complete** — All 14 original in-scope items + 1 follow-up item implemented. Build passes, 200/200 tests pass.

## Implementation Summary

| Item | Change | Files |
|------|--------|-------|
| Item 1 — Throws table | Added `renderExceptionTableMarkdown()`. Exception sections batched into a `\| Exception \| Description \|` table (same pattern as Parameters), using `katCommentStudio.typeName` color for exception name cells. Old per-exception `$(warning) Throws` header/block removed. | `commentRenderer.ts` |
| Item 2 — Reflow rename/split | Added `reflowCurrentComment()` that finds and reflows only the block containing the cursor, with info notification when cursor is outside a comment. Updated `reflowComment` command to use it. Added `onDidChangeTextEditorSelection` listener that sets `kat-comment-studio.cursorInXmlComment` context variable. Added `enablement` clause on the submenu entry. Renamed commands: `reflowComment` → "Reflow Current Comment", `reflowAllComments` → "Reflow Comments in File". | `reflowCommands.ts`, `extension.ts`, `package.json` |
| Item 3 — Remove cycleRenderingMode | Removed `cycleRenderingMode()` method from `DecorationManager`, removed command registration in `extension.ts`, removed command definition and submenu entry from `package.json`. | `decorationManager.ts`, `extension.ts`, `package.json` |
| Item 4 — XML-safe word wrap | Replaced `text.split(/\s+/)` in `wrapParagraph()` with `tokenizePreservingXml()` — a state-machine tokenizer that keeps all `<...>` sequences as single indivisible tokens, preventing XML tags from being split across lines during reflow. | `reflowEngine.ts` |
| Item 6 — Remove left border | Removed `LeftBorderMode` type, `leftBorder` field from `CommentStudioConfig`, all `leftBorder` references in `DecorationManager` and `DecorationFactory`, `leftBorder` configuration from `package.json`. `createDecorationStyles()` signature simplified to just `dimOpacity`. | `types.ts`, `decorationFactory.ts`, `decorationManager.ts`, `configuration.ts`, `package.json` |
| Item 7 — Line break rendering | Rewrote `sectionLinesToMarkdownRich()` to accumulate non-blank lines in a `paragraphTokens` buffer joined with spaces, flushing to `\n\n` only on blank lines. Eliminates forced `  \n` hard-breaks after every source line — prose now flows naturally. | `commentRenderer.ts` |
| Item 14 — Combined LINK: syntax warning | Added `invalidCombinedSyntax?: true` to `LinkAnchorTarget`. Parser sets this flag when both `lineNumber` and `anchorName` are parsed simultaneously. Validator emits a `Warning` diagnostic with code `invalid-link-syntax`. Navigator logs a console warning and navigates to anchor (anchor takes priority). | `linkAnchorParser.ts`, `linkValidator.ts`, `linkNavigator.ts` |
| Items 15/16 — Scope/filter icons | Changed `setAnchorScope` icon from `$(symbol-number)` to `$(surround-with)`. Changed `filterAnchorTypes` icon from `$(filter)` to `$(list-filter)`. Added `<span class="codicon codicon-surround-with toolbar-icon">` and `<span class="codicon codicon-list-filter toolbar-icon">` to web panel toolbar. Added `.toolbar-icon` CSS class. | `package.json`, `anchorsGridWebview.ts` |
| Item 17 — Status bar yellow | Replaced deprecated `problemsWarningIcon.foreground` ThemeColor with new contributed color `katCommentStudio.anchorCountForeground` (defaults to `panelTitle.activeForeground` across all themes). Contributed the color in `package.json` colors array. | `extension.ts`, `package.json` |
| Item 18 — Overdue warning icon | Replaced `createTextCell()` call for due date with inline cell construction that prepends `<span class="codicon codicon-warning">` when `isOverdue()` is true. | `anchorsGridWebview.ts` |
| Item 19 — Auto-bump version | Added `"package"` script: `npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && npm run compile && npx vsce package`. Uses `npx vsce` so no global install required. | `package.json` |
| Item 20 — CodeLens max length 0 | Changed truncation guard to `if (this.codeLensMaxLength > 0 && ...)`. Changed default from `100` to `0` in both `CommentCodeLensProvider` and `configuration.ts`. Updated `package.json` schema: `default: 0`, `minimum: 0`, updated description. | `commentCodeLensProvider.ts`, `configuration.ts`, `package.json` |
| Item 21 — `<br/>` and `<permission>` | Added `case 'br':` in `renderNode()` that pushes a blank `RenderedSectionLine`. Added `case 'permission':` in `renderTopLevelNode()` — extracts `cref`, creates an `Other` section with heading `$(lock) Permission`, renders the cref type name as a `TypeRef` segment followed by the description children. | `commentRenderer.ts` |
| Items 8/9/10 — Anchor colorize mode | Added `anchorColorizeMode: 'never' \| 'caseSensitive' \| 'caseInsensitive'` to `CommentStudioConfig` and `getConfiguration()`. Added `kat-comment-studio.anchorColorizeMode` setting in `package.json`. Updated `updateDecorations()` to check `HAS_COLON_RE` after each match: always colorize if colon present; standalone `ANCHOR` (no `(name):`) always skipped; other bare tags respect `colorizeMode`. | `anchorDecorationManager.ts`, `configuration.ts`, `package.json` |
| Follow-up — Anchor metadata highlight | Added `METADATA_RE` to extract `(…)` / `[…]` metadata groups. Added `metadataDecorationType` using `katCommentStudio.typeName` ThemeColor (the same light-blue type color used in hover popups). During `updateDecorations()`, when a tag matches and has a colon, any metadata group between the tag and the colon is collected into `metadataRanges` and decorated separately. `clearDecorations()` and `disposeDecorations()` updated accordingly. | `anchorDecorationManager.ts` |
| Link validation — anchor-before-line | `LINK: file.cs#Name:42` was silently producing a broken-file diagnostic because the anchor regex `/^[\w-]+$/` rejects `Name:42`, leaving `#Name` embedded in `targetPath` after line extraction. Fix: after lineMatch, scan `targetPath` for a residual `#anchor` and extract it. Both line + anchor → `invalidCombinedSyntax` → "can't combine" warning. | `linkAnchorParser.ts` |
| Link validation — missing file path | `LINK: :42` and `LINK: :42#anchor` gave a nonsensical "target not found: :42" error. Added `missingFilePath?: true` flag. After anchor/line extraction, if `targetPath` matches `/^:(\d+)/` (bare colon-number), line is extracted and `targetPath` set to `''`. Empty `targetPath` → `missingFilePath = true`. Validator emits a clear "missing file path" diagnostic (includes combined-syntax note when both flags set). | `linkAnchorParser.ts`, `linkValidator.ts` |
| Link validation — local anchor with line | `LINK: #first-anchor:41` silently ignored the `:41` suffix. Updated local anchor regex to `/^#([\w-]+)(:\d+)?/`. When suffix present, sets `invalidCombinedSyntax = true`. Validator updated to not skip local anchors when `invalidCombinedSyntax` is set. | `linkAnchorParser.ts`, `linkValidator.ts` |

## Documentation Notes

- **`anchorColorizeMode`** (new setting): Controls colorization of anchor keywords NOT followed by `:`. Values: `never`, `caseSensitive`, `caseInsensitive` (default). Keywords with a colon (e.g., `TODO:`, `HACK(owner):`) always colorize regardless of this setting. Standalone `ANCHOR` keyword is always skipped (it is only meaningful as `ANCHOR(name):`).
- **`codeLensMaxLength`**: Default changed from `100` to `0` (no truncation). Any existing user setting of `100` will now truncate at 100 chars as before; any explicit `0` or users who have not set it will see full summaries.
- **`katCommentStudio.anchorCountForeground`** (new color token): Allows theme authors and users to override the anchor status bar highlight color. Defaults to `panelTitle.activeForeground`.
- **`npm run package`**: Bumps the patch version in `package.json`, compiles, and creates a `.vsix`. The version bump is file-only (no git commit/tag). Run `git commit` manually after packaging if you want to track the version bump.
- **Reflow commands** rename: "Reflow Comment" → "Reflow Current Comment" (cursor-based), "Reflow All Comments" → "Reflow Comments in File". The right-click "Reflow Current Comment" item is grayed out when the cursor is not inside an XML doc comment.
- **Left border removed entirely**: The `kat-comment-studio.leftBorder` setting no longer exists. Any existing user setting referencing it will be silently ignored by VS Code.

## Overview

Items span four areas: XML comment rendering, reflow/edit commands, anchor system (grid panel and inline decorations), and extension packaging. Each item is analyzed with root cause and fix strategy.

---

## Group A — XML Comment Popup Rendering

### Item 1 — Throws Section in Table Format

**Request:** Render `<exception>` in the hover popup as a table (like Parameters).

**Current behavior:**
Each `<exception>` renders as a standalone block — header line `$(warning) Throws` followed by `sectionLinesToMarkdownRich()` output, one per exception, with no grouping:

```
// commentRenderer.ts:207-212
case CommentSectionType.Exception: {
  const excName = section.name || 'Exception';
  md.appendMarkdown(`<span ...>**$(warning) Throws**</span> <span ...>\`${excName}\`</span>\n\n`);
  md.appendMarkdown(sectionLinesToMarkdownRich(section));
  break;
}
```

**How Parameters do it:** All `Param` and `TypeParam` sections are collected at lines 158–175 and batched through `renderParamTableMarkdown()`, which produces a markdown table with a "Parameters" heading and one row per param. The loop then skips subsequent param sections (`if (paramSectionsRendered) continue`).

**Fix plan:**

1. Add `renderExceptionTableMarkdown(sections: RenderedCommentSection[]): string` alongside `renderParamTableMarkdown()` in `commentRenderer.ts`. Pattern is the same: heading + markdown table with `| Exception | Description |` columns. The exception name cell uses `--vscode-katCommentStudio-typeName` color (matches how type names are colored elsewhere).
2. In `renderToMarkdown()`, collect exception sections before the main loop (mirror the `paramSections` collect at line 158). Add `exceptionSectionsRendered` gate.
3. In the loop, when `section.type === CommentSectionType.Exception`, check `exceptionSectionsRendered`: if not yet rendered, emit the table and set the flag; otherwise `continue`.

**Files changed:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

### Item 7 — Line Breaks During Rendering (Reflowed Comments)

**Request:** When rendering a comment section for hover display, consecutive text lines should concatenate into a single flow of prose — let the Markdown container handle wrapping naturally. Only an explicit `<para>` element or a blank `///` line in the source should produce a paragraph break.

**Current behavior:**
`sectionLinesToMarkdownRich()` appends `'  \n'` (trailing two spaces = Markdown hard line break) after *every* rendered line:

```typescript
// commentRenderer.ts:309
parts.push(lineText + '  \n');
```

Each line of source XML (however it was wrapped by autoReflow or manual editing) becomes a forced line break in the hover popup, so a sentence reflowed across three lines renders as three visually broken lines. The user expected one seamless paragraph.

**Root cause:**
The `  \n` hard-break mirrors the source-file line structure rather than the logical prose structure. XML doc comments are prose; the source line boundaries are an editing artefact, not semantic breaks.

**Fix plan:**

Change `sectionLinesToMarkdownRich()` to accumulate non-blank lines by concatenating them with a single space (like joining words), and only emit a paragraph separator (`\n\n`) when a blank line is encountered:

```typescript
function sectionLinesToMarkdownRich(section: RenderedCommentSection): string {
  const parts: string[] = [];
  let paragraphTokens: string[] = [];    // accumulate current paragraph's text

  const flushParagraph = () => {
    if (paragraphTokens.length > 0) {
      parts.push(paragraphTokens.join(' ') + '\n\n');
      paragraphTokens = [];
    }
  };

  for (let i = 0; i < section.lines.length; i++) {
    const line = section.lines[i];

    if (isBlankLine(line)) {
      flushParagraph();   // blank /// line = paragraph break
      continue;
    }

    // Code-only lines: preserve as fenced block (existing logic, unchanged)
    if (line.segments.length === 1 && line.segments[0].type === SegmentType.Code) {
      // ... existing fenced-block accumulation ...
      continue;
    }

    // Normal prose line — append to current paragraph buffer
    const lineText = line.segments.map(s => segmentToMarkdownRich(s)).join('');
    paragraphTokens.push(lineText);
  }

  flushParagraph();
  return parts.join('');
}
```

The `<para>` handler in `renderNode()` already pushes blank `RenderedSectionLine` entries before and after its content, so `<para>` blocks will produce `\n\n` paragraph separators with no additional changes needed.

**Files changed:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts` (`sectionLinesToMarkdownRich`)

---

### Item 21 — Unhandled XML Elements in Popup Rendering

**Request:** Report which of the [C# XML doc recommended tags](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/xmldoc/recommended-tags) are NOT handled during popup rendering, and implement the missing ones.

**Current state analysis** (cross-referenced against the actual Microsoft docs link):

| Tag | Handled | Notes |
|-----|---------|-------|
| `<summary>` | ✅ | Top-level section |
| `<remarks>` | ✅ | Top-level section |
| `<returns>` | ✅ | Top-level section |
| `<value>` | ✅ | Top-level section |
| `<example>` | ✅ | Top-level section |
| `<param>` | ✅ | Batched into Parameter table |
| `<typeparam>` | ✅ | Batched into Parameter table |
| `<exception>` | ✅ | Rendered (see Item 1 to convert to table) |
| `<seealso>` | ✅ | Top-level section |
| `<inheritdoc>` | ✅ | Top-level section |
| `<see>` | ✅ | Inline link |
| `<paramref>` | ✅ | Inline colored backtick |
| `<typeparamref>` | ✅ | Inline colored backtick |
| `<c>` | ✅ | Inline code |
| `<code>` | ✅ | Fenced code block |
| `<para>` | ✅ | Paragraph break |
| `<list>` | ✅ | Renders list items |
| `<b>` / `<strong>` | ✅ | Bold |
| `<i>` / `<em>` | ✅ | Italic |
| `<br/>` | ❌ | Falls through `default:` — tag ignored. Should insert a line break |
| `<permission>` | ❌ | Official MS recommended tag — falls through `default:`, no section heading |

**Fix plan:**

1. Add explicit `case 'br':` in `renderNode()` that pushes a new blank line to the current section:
   ```typescript
   case 'br':
     section.lines.push({ segments: [] });
     break;
   ```
2. Add a `<permission>` top-level handler in `renderTopLevelNode()`. Extract the `cref` attribute as the permission target name, render as a section with heading `$(lock) Permission` and description text beneath (same shape as the `<returns>` section).
3. The `default:` fallback already renders children for all other unrecognised tags, so no additional changes needed for non-official tags.

**Files changed:** `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts`

---

## Group B — Reflow and Edit Commands

### Item 2 — Confusing Reflow Command Names

**Request:** Rename "Reflow All Comments" and split commands clearly into "Reflow Comments in File" and "Reflow Current Comment". The "Reflow Current Comment" right-click item should be disabled (grayed out) when the cursor is not inside an XML doc comment, rather than silently doing nothing.

**Current behavior:**
Both registered commands call the identical function body (see original plan). Neither detects cursor position. `ReflowCodeActionProvider` already has the correct cursor-in-block detection logic (it only offers the code action when the cursor is inside a block) — this just needs to be wired to the right-click context menu via a `when`/`enablement` clause.

**Fix plan:**

1. **Reflow Current Comment:** Change `kat-comment-studio.reflowComment` to use cursor-position-based logic: find the block containing the cursor, reflow only that block. If cursor is not in a block, show an information notification.

2. **Context variable for menu enablement:** Register an `onDidChangeTextEditorSelection` listener that calls `findAllCommentBlocks()` for the active editor and sets a VS Code context variable:
   ```typescript
   const inComment = blocks.some(b => cursorLine >= b.startLine && cursorLine <= b.endLine);
   void vscode.commands.executeCommand('setContext', 'kat-comment-studio.cursorInXmlComment', inComment);
   ```

3. **`enablement` on the menu item in `package.json`:** Add an `enablement` property to the `reflowComment` entry in every menu group where it appears. VS Code will render the item grayed out when `cursorInXmlComment` is false, instead of hiding it:
   ```json
   {
     "command": "kat-comment-studio.reflowComment",
     "group": "2_reflow@1",
     "enablement": "kat-comment-studio.cursorInXmlComment"
   }
   ```

4. **Reflow Comments in File:** Rename the `kat-comment-studio.reflowAllComments` title in `package.json` from `"Reflow All Comments"` to `"Reflow Comments in File"`. No logic change — it already works on the current file.

**Files changed:** `src/extension.ts`, `src/reflow/reflowCommands.ts` (add `reflowCurrentComment()` helper), `package.json`

---

### Item 3 — Toggle Rendering vs Cycle Rendering Mode (Duplicate Commands)

**Request:** Right-click shows "Toggle Comment Rendering" and "Cycle Rendering Mode (Off → On)" — both seem to do the same thing. Clarify or remove the duplicate.

**Current behavior — they ARE identical:**

```typescript
// decorationManager.ts:88-100
toggleRendering(): void {
  if (this.config.renderingMode === 'off') setRenderingMode('on');
  else setRenderingMode('off');
}

cycleRenderingMode(): void {
  const modes: RenderingMode[] = ['off', 'on'];          // ← only 2 modes
  const currentIndex = modes.indexOf(this.config.renderingMode);
  setRenderingMode(modes[(currentIndex + 1) % modes.length]);
}
```

With only two modes in the array, cycling produces the same on/off toggle as `toggleRendering`. There is no third mode (e.g., `'dim'`) to cycle through.

**Fix plan:**

1. Remove `kat-comment-studio.cycleRenderingMode` from `package.json` commands, context menu (`editor/context`), and submenu.
2. Remove `cycleRenderingMode()` method from `decorationManager.ts`.
3. Remove the command registration in `extension.ts` (line 130–132).
4. Keep `toggleRendering` as the sole on/off command. The status bar item already invokes `toggleRendering`.

**Files changed:** `src/rendering/decorationManager.ts`, `src/extension.ts`, `package.json`

---

### Item 4 — Reflow Must Not Split XML Self-Closing Tags

**Request:** Reflow must never break `<paramref name="desiredDay"/>` or any XML inline element across lines.

**Current behavior:**
`XML_SELF_CLOSE_TAG_REGEX` only matches a line that is *entirely* a self-closing tag (i.e., `^\s*<(\w+)([^>]*)\s*\/>\s*$`). When a `<paramref .../>` is inline with surrounding text (e.g., `"Pass <paramref name="desiredDay"/> to set the day."`), `reflowXmlContent()` classifies the whole line as free text content and passes it to `wrapParagraph()`.

Inside `wrapParagraph()`:
```typescript
const words = text.split(/\s+/).filter(w => w);
```

`<paramref` and `name="desiredDay"/>` are two separate words split at the space inside the attribute. When the wrapping threshold is reached mid-tag, the wrap point can fall between `<paramref` and `name=...>`, producing:
```
/// <paramref
/// name="desiredDay"/> to set the day.
```

**Fix plan:**

The `wrapParagraph()` tokenizer needs to treat XML tags as atomic units. Replace the simple whitespace split with a smarter tokenizer:

1. In `reflowEngine.ts`, replace `text.split(/\s+/)` in `wrapParagraph()` with a regex that splits on whitespace BUT keeps any `<...>` sequence as a single indivisible token:
   ```typescript
   // Split on whitespace except inside XML tag brackets
   const words = tokenizePreservingXml(text);
   ```
2. Implement `tokenizePreservingXml(text: string): string[]` using a simple state machine: scan left-to-right, accumulate a buffer; when `<` is encountered, keep accumulating (including spaces) until the matching `>`.

**Files changed:** `src/reflow/reflowEngine.ts`

---

### Item 5 — ~~Reflow Boundary Column Marker Decoration~~

**Removed from scope.** Not worth the implementation complexity — VS Code lacks a per-file column decoration API and the only viable path (mutating `editor.rulers`) touches workspace config.

## Group C — Extension and Configuration Cleanup

### Item 6 — Remove Left Border Decoration Completely

**Request:** Remove the left border decoration from all implementation, configuration, and documentation.

**All locations:**

| Location | What to remove |
|----------|---------------|
| `src/types.ts` | `LeftBorderMode` type |
| `src/rendering/decorationFactory.ts` | `leftBorder` field in `DecorationStyles`, entire conditional block |
| `src/rendering/decorationManager.ts` | `leftBorderDecorations` array, all `this.styles.leftBorder` references (~5 locations) |
| `src/configuration.ts` | `leftBorder` field in config read |
| `package.json` | `kat-comment-studio.leftBorder` setting definition, `kat-comment-studio.leftBorderColor` color contribution |

**Files changed:** `src/types.ts`, `src/rendering/decorationFactory.ts`, `src/rendering/decorationManager.ts`, `src/configuration.ts`, `package.json`

---

### Item 19 — Auto-Bump Revision on Package Generation

**Request:** When generating a `.vsix` package, automatically bump the patch version number in `package.json` before compiling.

**Current behavior:** `package.json` has no `package` script. Packaging requires manually running `vsce package` and manually bumping the version.

**Fix plan:**

Add a `package` script to `package.json` that:
1. Uses `npm version patch --no-git-tag-version` to bump `package.json` version in place (no git commit, no tag — just the file write).
2. Runs `npm run compile`.
3. Runs `vsce package`.

```json
"scripts": {
  "package": "npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && npm run compile && vsce package"
}
```

**Note:** `vsce` must be installed as a dev dependency or globally. If it's not present, add `"@vscode/vsce": "^3.0.0"` to `devDependencies` and reference it as `npx vsce package`.

**Files changed:** `package.json`

---

## Group D — Anchor System

### Items 8/9/10 — Anchor Keyword Colorization in Editor

**Request:**
- Item 9: Skip `ANCHOR` keyword from inline colorization **when standalone** (bare `ANCHOR` without metadata or colon). `ANCHOR(name):` still colorizes — it has a name and a colon.
- Item 10: Add a `colorizeMode` option for non-`:` anchor keywords (Never / Case Sensitive / Case Insensitive). Keywords followed by `:` (or `(...):`  / `[...]:` with metadata) always colorize regardless.

**Current behavior:**
`anchorDecorationManager.updateDecorations()` searches for each tag name as a case-insensitive word boundary match anywhere in the comment, regardless of `:` suffix or metadata tokens.

**Colon detection must account for metadata tokens:**
The anchor syntax allows metadata before the colon: `TAG(owner, #issue, 2025-01-01): description` or `TAG[tokens]:`. A keyword "has a colon" if, after the tag name, what follows matches the pattern `(\s*[\(\[][^\)\]]*[\)\]])?:` — i.e., optional `(...)` or `[...]` group then `:`.

**Specific ANCHOR rule:**
- `ANCHOR` with NO `(name):` → skip (standalone, not a named anchor)
- `ANCHOR(name):` → colorize (has metadata + colon — it IS a named anchor)
- This is consistent with how ANCHOR is parsed: `anchorService.ts:122–123` skips ANCHOR if no `anchorName`

**Fix plan:**

1. **Item 9 — Standalone ANCHOR skip:** In `updateDecorations()`, after finding an ANCHOR match, check if it's immediately followed by `(` (start of name) or `:`. If it's a bare `ANCHOR` word with nothing after it (or followed by a space/end), skip. Otherwise (has `(name):`) treat like any other tag.

2. **Item 10 — colorizeMode config:** Add `kat-comment-studio.anchorColorizeMode` to `package.json`:
   ```json
   "kat-comment-studio.anchorColorizeMode": {
     "type": "string",
     "enum": ["never", "caseSensitive", "caseInsensitive"],
     "default": "caseInsensitive",
     "description": "Controls colorization of anchor keywords in editor comments when NOT followed by ':' (or '(...):' / '[...]:' with metadata). Keywords with a colon always colorize."
   }
   ```

3. **In `updateDecorations()`:** For each match, check if the text following the tag name matches `\s*(?:[\(\[][^\)\]]*[\)\]])?\s*:` (has-colon-or-metadata-colon pattern):
   - If yes → always colorize
   - If no → apply `colorizeMode`:
     - `'never'`: skip
     - `'caseSensitive'`: only colorize if source text case matches the tag definition exactly
     - `'caseInsensitive'`: colorize regardless of case (current behavior for bare tags)

4. **In `configuration.ts`:** Add `anchorColorizeMode` to config interface and getter.

**Files changed:** `src/anchors/anchorDecorationManager.ts`, `src/configuration.ts`, `package.json`

---

### Item 14 — Disallow Combined Line + Anchor Syntax in LINK:

**Request:** Keep `:lineNum` navigation AND `#anchorName` navigation as separate features. Only the **combined** form `LINK: file.cs:42#AnchorName` (specifying both simultaneously) is invalid. Add a diagnostic squiggle on that specific form with a descriptive message. When both are present the current default is to navigate to the anchor.

**Current behavior:**
`linkAnchorParser.ts:parseLinkTarget()` parses suffixes in sequence — first strips `#anchor` (if present), then strips `:lineNum` (if present on the remainder). Both can be non-null simultaneously. There is no validation that they are mutually exclusive.

**Combined form detection:**
After parsing, when `lineNumber !== undefined && anchorName !== undefined` — both were extracted from the same target string.

**Fix plan:**

1. **`linkAnchorParser.ts`:** After both suffix parses (lines 110–140), detect the combined form:
   ```typescript
   if (lineNumber !== undefined && anchorName !== undefined) {
     return {
       rawText: `LINK: ${pathPart}`,
       targetPath: targetPath.trim(),
       lineNumber,
       endLineNumber,
       anchorName,
       isLocalAnchor: false,
       pathStart: linkStart + prefixLength,
       pathLength: pathPart.length,
       invalidCombinedSyntax: true,   // new flag
     };
   }
   ```
   Add `invalidCombinedSyntax?: true` to the `LinkAnchorTarget` interface.

2. **`linkValidator.ts`:** In `validateDocument()`, after the existing file-exists check, also check for `invalidCombinedSyntax`:
   ```typescript
   if (target.invalidCombinedSyntax) {
     const range = new vscode.Range(lineNum, target.pathStart, lineNum, target.pathStart + target.pathLength);
     diagnostics.push(new vscode.Diagnostic(
       range,
       'LINK syntax error: cannot specify both a line number and an anchor name. Use file.cs:42 or file.cs#AnchorName.',
       vscode.DiagnosticSeverity.Warning,
     ));
   }
   ```

3. **`linkNavigator.ts`:** When `target.invalidCombinedSyntax` is true, navigate to the anchor (anchor takes priority) and log a console warning. No change to normal line-only or anchor-only navigation paths.

**Files changed:** `src/navigation/linkAnchorParser.ts`, `src/navigation/linkValidator.ts`, `src/navigation/linkNavigator.ts`

---

### Item 15 — Scope Icon in Tree and Web Panel

**Request:** Change the scope button icon from `symbol-number` to `surround-with` in both the tree pane header and web panel toolbar.

**Current behavior:**
`package.json` command definition for `kat-comment-studio.setAnchorScope` uses `$(symbol-number)` as its icon (line 196). The web panel (`anchorsGridWebview.ts`) does not currently show this icon at all.

**Fix plan:**

1. **`package.json`:** Change the icon from `$(symbol-number)` to `$(surround-with)`:
   ```json
   {
     "command": "kat-comment-studio.setAnchorScope",
     "title": "Set Anchor Scope",
     "icon": "$(surround-with)"
   }
   ```
   This applies automatically to the tree pane `view/title` button since the tree item references the command.

2. **`anchorsGridWebview.ts`:** Add the `codicon-surround-with` icon to the scope select element in the toolbar HTML:
   ```html
   <span class="codicon codicon-surround-with toolbar-icon" title="Scope"></span>
   <select class="scope-select" id="scopeSelect"></select>
   ```

**Files changed:** `package.json`, `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`

---

### Item 16 — Filter Icon in Tree and Web Panel

**Request:** Change the filter button icon from `filter` to `list-filter` in both the tree pane header and web panel toolbar.

**Current behavior:**
`package.json` command `kat-comment-studio.filterAnchorTypes` uses `$(filter)` (line 202). No filter icon is shown in the web panel.

**Fix plan:**

1. **`package.json`:** Change icon from `$(filter)` to `$(list-filter)`:
   ```json
   {
     "command": "kat-comment-studio.filterAnchorTypes",
     "title": "Filter Anchor Types",
     "icon": "$(list-filter)"
   }
   ```

2. **`anchorsGridWebview.ts`:** Add the `codicon-list-filter` icon before the search input in the toolbar HTML:
   ```html
   <span class="codicon codicon-list-filter toolbar-icon" title="Filter by type"></span>
   <input type="text" class="search-input" id="searchInput" placeholder="Filter anchors..." />
   ```
   Combine CSS for `.toolbar-icon` from Item 15.

**Files changed:** `package.json`, `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`

---

### Item 17 — Status Bar Anchor Count in Yellow

**Request:** The `# Anchors` status bar item should display in yellow — specifically matching the VS Code active panel tab title color (the yellow of "TERMINAL" when active, as shown in the screenshot). Only apply yellow when anchors are present AND the web panel has not yet been opened (current behavior, unchanged).

**Current behavior:**
```typescript
// extension.ts:326
const warningForeground = new vscode.ThemeColor('problemsWarningIcon.foreground');
anchorStatusBarItem.color = anchorStatusBarHighlighted ? warningForeground : undefined;
```

**Why it hasn't worked:** `StatusBarItem.color` is deprecated in VS Code 1.90+ for default-kind items. In newer versions the property is silently ignored. `problemsWarningIcon.foreground` also does not reliably resolve to yellow across all themes.

**Target color:** In the screenshot the yellow "TERMINAL" text is the active panel title. That is the `panelTitle.activeForeground` theme color in VS Code. This is theme-specific (the screenshot shows a custom theme with yellow active panel titles) — using this token exactly matches whatever the user's theme defines for that element.

**Fix plan:**

1. Contribute a custom theme color `katCommentStudio.anchorCountForeground` in `package.json`, defaulting to `panelTitle.activeForeground` for all three themes:
   ```json
   {
     "id": "katCommentStudio.anchorCountForeground",
     "description": "Foreground color of the anchor count status bar item when anchors are present and unviewed. Defaults to the active panel title color.",
     "defaults": {
       "dark": "panelTitle.activeForeground",
       "light": "panelTitle.activeForeground",
       "highContrast": "panelTitle.activeForeground"
     }
   }
   ```

2. In `extension.ts`, replace the `warningForeground` reference:
   ```typescript
   const anchorHighlightColor = new vscode.ThemeColor('katCommentStudio.anchorCountForeground');
   anchorStatusBarItem.color = anchorStatusBarHighlighted ? anchorHighlightColor : undefined;
   ```

3. `StatusBarItem.color` still works for simple text coloring in VS Code — the deprecation only affects `backgroundColor` patterns. Using a contributed ThemeColor via `.color` is the documented pattern for custom status bar text color.

**Files changed:** `src/extension.ts`, `package.json`

---

### Item 18 — Warning Codicon on Overdue Due Dates in Grid

**Request:** In the due date column, when a date is past due, also show a `$(warning)` codicon in addition to the existing red color.

**Current behavior:**
```typescript
// anchorsGridWebview.ts:780
row.appendChild(createTextCell(
  anchor.dueDate ? formatLocaleDate(anchor.dueDate) : '', 
  'dueDate', 
  anchor.dueDate || '', 
  isOverdue(anchor.dueDate) ? 'overdue' : '',
));
```
The `overdue` CSS class turns the text red and bold. No icon is shown.

The tree pane tooltip already uses `$(warning)` codicon — that implementation is a reference.

**Fix plan:**

Modify the due date cell construction to prepend a warning codicon span when overdue:

```javascript
// In the row-building section of anchorsGridWebview.ts
const dueDateDisplay = anchor.dueDate ? formatLocaleDate(anchor.dueDate) : '';
const dueDateCell = document.createElement('td');
dueDateCell.dataset.col = 'dueDate';
dueDateCell.dataset.sort = anchor.dueDate || '';
if (isOverdue(anchor.dueDate)) {
  dueDateCell.className = 'overdue';
  const icon = document.createElement('span');
  icon.className = 'codicon codicon-warning';
  icon.style.marginRight = '4px';
  dueDateCell.appendChild(icon);
  dueDateCell.appendChild(document.createTextNode(dueDateDisplay));
} else {
  dueDateCell.textContent = dueDateDisplay;
}
```

**Files changed:** `src/anchors/anchorsGridWebview.ts`

---

### Item 20 — CodeLens Max Length: Zero/Blank = No Truncation

**Request:** If `codeLensMaxLength` is set to 0 or left blank, disable truncation entirely. Change default to 0 (no truncation).

**Current behavior:**
```typescript
// commentCodeLensProvider.ts:102-104
const summary = rawSummary.length > this.codeLensMaxLength
  ? rawSummary.substring(0, this.codeLensMaxLength) + '...'
  : rawSummary;
```
Default is `100` (from `configuration.ts:40`). There is no special case for 0.

**Fix plan:**

1. Change truncation guard: `if (this.codeLensMaxLength > 0 && rawSummary.length > this.codeLensMaxLength)`
2. Change default in `configuration.ts` from `100` to `0`.
3. Change `package.json` schema:
   - Update `default` to `0`
   - Update `description` to: `"Maximum number of characters for the CodeLens summary text before truncation. Set to 0 for no truncation (default)."`
   - Add `"minimum": 0` to the JSON schema.

**Files changed:** `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts`, `src/configuration.ts`, `package.json`
